content.problemStatement:
Chef got into a fight with the evil Dr Doof. Dr Doof has decided to destroy all even numbers from the universe using his Evil-Destroy-inator. Chef has NNN integers with him. To stop Doof, Chef has to find an odd number which is an integer multiple of all NNN numbers that he has with him. Find if it is possible for Chef to prevent Dr Doof from destroying the even numbers.

Formally, given NNN positive integers, find if there exists an odd number which is an integer multiple of all the given NNN numbers. If yes, print **"YES"**, otherwise **"NO"**. You can print any letter in any case.

###Input


First line contains TTT, number of testcases. Each testcase consists of 222 lines.
The first line of each test case consists of a positive integer NNN, denoting the number of positive integers Chef has.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers AiA_iAi‚Äã each denoting an integer that Chef has with him.
###Output

For every test case, if there exists such an odd number, print **"YES"** on a separate line, otherwise **"NO"**. The judge is case insensitive. That means, your code can print any letter in any case ( **"Yes"**, **"yes"** or **"YES"** are all accepted).

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1031 \leq T \leq 10^31‚â§T‚â§103
1‚â§N‚â§1031 \leq N \leq 10^31‚â§N‚â§103
1‚â§Ai‚â§1031 \leq A_i \leq 10^31‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§103

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For test 111: There exists no odd number.
For test 222: The possible odd numbers can be 777, 212121, 494949, 315315315, ...
